description: Captures leads from multiple sources (Facebook, Google, Website), creates unified lead records, and tracks each lead through a defined lifecycle from first contact to enrollment or drop-off.

screens.description: Displays all leads in Kanban board or table view with filtering and sorting capabilities

features.description: Visual board with columns for each lead status (New, Contacted, Trial Scheduled, Trial Attended, Enrolled, Dropped)

requirements.description: Lead cards can be dragged between status columns to update status
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Dragging a card from 'New' to 'Contacted' updates the lead status in the database and reflects immediately in the UI

requirements.description: Each card displays parent name, child age, source, and interested program
requirements.acceptance_criteria: All four fields are visible on the card without expanding it

features.description: Tabular list of all leads with sortable columns

requirements.description: Table displays columns: Parent Name, Email, Phone, Child Age, Source, Program, Status, Date Created
requirements.acceptance_criteria: All columns are visible and each column is sortable in ascending/descending order

features.description: Filter leads by source, status, and program

requirements.description: Admin can filter leads by source (Facebook, Google, Website), status (any lifecycle stage), and interested program
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Applying a filter immediately updates the visible leads; multiple filters can be combined; filters can be cleared individually or all at once

screens.description: Detailed view of a single lead with all information, notes, and activity history

features.description: Full lead information display and edit

requirements.description: Display all lead fields: parent name, email, phone, child age, source, interested program, status, date created
requirements.acceptance_criteria: All fields are displayed and editable by admin

requirements.description: Admin can manually change lead status to any lifecycle stage
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Status dropdown shows all stages; selecting a new status saves immediately

features.description: Admin can add timestamped notes to a lead record

requirements.description: Admin can add free-text notes to any lead; each note is timestamped and attributed to the admin user
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Notes appear in reverse chronological order; each note shows text, timestamp, and author

features.description: Chronological log of all actions taken on this lead (status changes, emails sent, notes added)

requirements.description: System automatically logs status changes, communications sent, and enrollment events for each lead
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Timeline shows entries for each logged event with timestamp, event type, and details

business_rules.rule: Leads from Facebook, Google, and Website sources are auto-created with source field populated
business_rules.acceptance_criteria: When a lead form is submitted from any integrated source, a lead record is created within 5 minutes with the correct source tag

business_rules.rule: Lead status auto-advances: booking a trial sets status to 'Trial Scheduled'; attending trial sets 'Trial Attended'; completing enrollment sets 'Enrolled'
business_rules.acceptance_criteria: Each enrollment event triggers the corresponding status change without admin intervention; admin can still override

business_rules.rule: Duplicate leads (same email) are merged or flagged rather than creating separate records
business_rules.acceptance_criteria: When a new lead arrives with an email matching an existing lead, system either merges data into the existing record or alerts admin of the duplicate

business_rules.rule: Lead lifecycle stages are: New Lead → Contacted → Trial Scheduled → Trial Attended → Enrolled → Dropped / Not Interested
business_rules.acceptance_criteria: Only these stages are available in the system; status transitions are logged in the activity timeline
